profile_basis.source: post-pass189 RCH linalg profile recorded in bead description
profile_basis.head: b997be19

lever.description: Pack the pc-major B panel block by scanning each source B row once and writing the same packed layout, instead of walking panel-by-panel and rereading distant row segments.
lever.layout_contract: Output pack remains [column_panel][kc][NR] inside each pc block; only source traversal order changes.

provisional_cross_worker.command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- linalg/matmul_2d_2048x2048x2048_f64
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- linalg/matmul_2d_2048x2048x2048_f64

isomorphism_proof: The packed buffer index for every source B element is unchanged: dst = jp * (kc * NR) + l * NR + lane.
The blocked GEMM loop order and C tile traversal remain pc -> row-superpanel -> column-superpanel -> tile -> jp -> l.
Every output element still accumulates the same products in strictly ascending k order.
No FMA request, mixed precision, reassociation, Strassen recursion, tolerance relaxation, RNG, tie-breaking, dtype, shape, or error behavior changed.
Thread row ownership and output row-major layout are unchanged.

rch_clippy.note: Local uncommitted peer-owned arithmetic.rs imported rank2_i64_matmul and triggered an unused-import warning; this pass stages only the B-pack hunk.

local.note: No critical findings; known broad warning inventory remains.

next_route: If exact-order GEMM remains dominant, attack a fundamentally different KC-slab streaming B-pack or a separately approved tolerance-contract primitive; do not repeat row-morsel scheduling.
